text: Vendor alert: Greenfield up in the Hudson Valley has early peaches dropping next week and the heirlooms are coming in hard. First pick is 6am Saturday.
Already on it, building a stone-fruit special around those peaches. Worth the drive every time.
Cross-post: Hollow Brook's chanterelles are flushing early. Members reporting strong color and yield.
Sourcing tip request forwarded to member inbox: looking for a reliable lamb supplier, quality-focused. Post replies in-thread.
PSA for anyone hunting space: get a quote on the hood and exhaust BEFORE you fall in love with the square footage.
Felt this in my soul. Looking at a 40-seat spot, hood needs work and I know it's going to be a five-figure surprise.
Community wisdom: if the bones are good it's worth it. Bones you can't fix. A hood you can.
Weekly technique thread: ounce of dark chocolate in a short rib braise. Genius or heresy?
Genius. It's the move. My grandmother's been doing it for 60 years and I only just got her to admit it. Adds depth, not sweetness.
Consensus building. Community reply tally: 8 for, 1 against. 70% cocoa recommended, not sweet chocolate.
New listing: 8-burner range and a 2-door reach-in, both maintained. Pickup Jersey City. DM the poster.
What's the ask on the reach-in? Might be exactly what I need down the line.
Hot take: jarred pre-chopped garlic should be a fireable offense. That's it, that's the post.
Community poll pinned: 94% agree. Microplane recommended over jarred by the room.
New pop-up post: seasonal tasting collab next month, Hudson Valley produce, 6 courses, looking for a pastry collaborator. Reply to signal interest.
Job board update: line cook role in Hoboken, nights and weekends, fair pay. Follow the board link to post references.
Reminder for everyone closing doubles this weekend: drink water, eat something that isn't staff meal, and take Monday for yourself. The line will survive.
Needed this. Closing Friday and Saturday both. Monday's sacred, that's family and my friend at the Copper Still.
Welcome to the new folks who joined this week. Drop your city, your station, and your most controversial food opinion.
Weekly technique digest: laminating in high humidity — drop your dough temp and walk away. Members reported success with this method last week.
